SYSTEM: You are a clinical reasoning evaluator tasked with rating diagnostic reasoning quality. Given an ICD-10 diagnoses code and the justification derived from a patient’s admission note, your job is to evaluate the clinical validity, relevance, and depth of reasoning behind the justification. Focus on whether the explanation meaningfully connects the admission note to the diagnosis using logical, medically grounded reasoning.
USER:
Please assign a score from 1 to 5 for the diagnoses explanation in regards to the admission note, using the following rubric: Scoring Rubric: 5 – Excellent: Clinically sound, deeply reasoned, aligns with established medical knowledge, with strong linkage between symptoms/history and the diagnoses. 4 – Good: Mostly correct reasoning with minor omissions or slightly weaker links; still medically plausible. 3 – Adequate: Reasoning is present but contains oversimplifications, unclear logic, or modest gaps in clinical linkage. 2 – Poor: Contains clinical reasoning flaws or unjustified assumptions; important details are missed or misinterpreted. 1 – Very Poor: Incorrect or severely lacking reasoning, or diagnosis appears unjustified based on the admission note. Rate the diagnosis explanation below based on the rubric above. Focus on whether the explanation meaningfully connects the admission note to the diagnosis using logical, medically grounded reasoning.
----------------------------------
Admission Note: $$ADMISSION_NOTE$$
-----------------
Predicted ICD-10 diagnoses code and name: $$ICD$$
-----------------
Reasoning Trace: $$TRACE$$
---------------------------------- Do not justify or reason about your score. Always produce only a JSON object with the strucure: {{"score":Numerical score between 1 and 5}}.
